DSDS 0.1
Overview Root Schema
example extensions link metadata presentation purpose rich-text status
component pattern style theme token
accessibility anatomy api best-practice content design-specifications guideline interaction motion principle scale state variant

Design System Documentation Standard (DSDS) v0.1

A standard, machine-readable format for design system documentation covering components, design tokens, token groups, themes, styles, and patterns. A DSDS document contains one or more documentation groups — named collections of typed entities. Each entity carries a `kind` discriminator that identifies it as a component, token, token-group, theme, style, or pattern. Entity schemas are defined in entities/. Guideline schemas are defined in guidelines/. Common schemas are defined in common/.

Source: dsds.schema.json

Root Properties

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
dsdsVersion"0.1"requiredThe version of the DSDS specification this document conforms to.
documentationdocumentationGroup[]requiredOne or more documentation groups. Each group is a named collection of typed entities — components, tokens, token groups, themes, styles, and/or patterns. Multiple groups allow a single DSDS file to organize artifacts into logical sections (e.g., one group for foundations, another for components).
Min items: 1
$schemastringoptionalURI reference to the DSDS JSON Schema for validation.
metadatametadataoptional
$extensionsextensionsoptional

documentationGroup

A named collection of design system documentation entities. Contains a single `items` array of typed entities — components, tokens, token groups, themes, styles, and/or patterns mixed freely in any order. Each entity is identified by its `kind` discriminator property. At least one entity must be present.

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
namestringrequiredHuman-readable name of the collection (e.g., 'Acme Design System', 'Color Tokens', 'Button Documentation').
itemscomponent | token | tokenGroup | theme | style | pattern[]requiredThe entities in this documentation group. Each item is a typed entity — its `kind` property determines which schema applies. Entities of different types can be mixed freely in the same array. The ordering is significant: tools SHOULD preserve it for display. Authors MAY organize entities in whatever order best serves their documentation (e.g., tokens before components, or grouped by domain).
Min items: 1
descriptionrichTextoptional
$extensionsextensionsoptional

References: richText, component, token, tokenGroup, theme, style, pattern, extensions
